words.hint: European cultural rebirth period after Middle Ages
words.educational: The Renaissance was a period of renewed interest in art, science, and learning in Europe (14th-17th century), marking the transition to modern times.
The Industrial Revolution (late 1700s-1800s) transformed society through mechanized manufacturing, factories, and mass production.

words.hint: Government by the people through voting
words.educational: Democracy is a form of government where power rests with the people. It originated in ancient Athens and has evolved over centuries.
The Crusades were a series of religious wars in the medieval period, primarily between Christians and Muslims over control of holy lands.
Gladiators were armed fighters who competed in violent confrontations for public entertainment in ancient Rome, often fighting to the death.

words.hint: Government ruled by a king or queen
words.educational: Monarchy is a form of government with a monarch (king or queen) as head of state. It was the most common government system throughout history.
Samurai were the military nobility of medieval Japan, following a code of honor called Bushido and known for their swordsmanship.
The Reformation (16th century) was a religious movement led by Martin Luther that challenged Catholic Church authority and created Protestantism.

words.hint: Policy of extending country power and influence
words.educational: Imperialism is when a country extends its power through colonization or military force. European imperialism shaped much of modern world history.
The abolition movement sought to end slavery. It succeeded in the British Empire (1833) and United States (1865) after long struggles.
